_risk_level_doc: medium because: (i) touches agents/qa.md verification step and commands/dev.md orchestrator dispatch — any markdown or JSON syntax break ripples into every downstream cycle, but (ii) all 8 changes are explicitly prescribed by spec Section 7 with no novel logic, and (iii) the regression target (Cycle 1's 18 ACs) is well-defined and spot-checkable. Not 'high' because there is no security-sensitive infrastructure change and the change set does not touch a hot pipeline path the user is mid-using.

requirement.original: 以上全部计划（研究对话中设计的所有组件），按优先级实施 — see spec-20260518-225715.md Section 5
requirement.clarified: Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715 closes the 3 /close-gate failure axes captured in spec Section 6, by executing Section 7's Cycle 1 → Cycle 2 remediation plan Priorities 1-3 only. P4 deferred to Cycle 3 per user decision during pre-dispatch clarification.
requirement.what: 8 prescribed remediations: (P1.1) add test-writer role to tool-policy.v1.json; (P1.2) reconcile manifest contract to Option (b) — per-task active_tests + root index — across test-writer.md + qa.md + commands/dev.md; (P2.3) append 3 patterns to .gitignore; (P2.4) regenerate README/INDEX/.claude/INDEX to drop leaked entries; (P3.6) prefix venv-activation to 5 python3 sites; (P3.7) renumber Sub-step 12.1 to Step 12a + update cross-ref; (P3.8) replace hardcoded /tmp/ in canary-verify.sh with mktemp -d + EXIT trap; (P3.9) add narrow Standard 6 exemption clause for user-facing source-language strings.
requirement.why: Cycle 1 delivered all 18 ACs PASS but /close gate detected 3 independent failure axes that per-AC verification did not surface: (i) downstream consumability — test-writer cannot be invoked on first dispatch (tool-policy missing + manifest drift); (ii) cleanliness — 3 runtime artifact patterns leaking into git; (iii) style — 12 critical style-inspector findings + 1 Standard 6 Chinese-language escalation. Cycle 2 closes all three to make /close grant YES.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Cycle 1 delivered 18 passing ACs but /close gate returned CLOSE:NO with 3 concurring failure axes captured in spec Section 6.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Three orthogonal Cycle-1 gaps that per-AC verification (Step 11) does not see but /close (Step 2 four-bullet rule + inspectors) does: (1) test-writer was specified but never verified to be invokable — tool-policy.v1 missing role + manifest contract drift between test-writer.md and qa.md; (2) runtime telemetry artifacts (agent-scores.json, .lock, .claude/specs/spec-*/) were not in .gitignore — auto-regenerated indices then leaked entries; (3) script venv-activation, decimal step numbering, hardcoded /tmp/, and Standard 6 Chinese-language strings were violations style-inspector flagged.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: Cycle 1 implementation (docs/dev/dev-report-20260519-132417.json era); no single commit hash because all 3 axes were introduced together in the Cycle 1 work product.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Cycle 1 BA spec covered per-component delivery (5.1-5.6) but did not include cross-component integration verification or repo-hygiene checks in its AC set. The 18 ACs were component-correct in isolation but missed integration boundaries (test-writer first-invocation policy + manifest cross-file shape) and platform-hygiene boundaries (gitignore, style standards).
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Without /close-gate satisfaction, the work cannot land in main. The user explicitly invoked /dev to close Cycle 2.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Cycle 1 finished 2026-05-19; /close gate fired same day, identified 3 axes; user dispatched Cycle 2 2026-05-19T21:15:15Z (TASK-ID 20260519-211515).

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Fail-closed default. Any new role MUST be explicitly defined; missing role = deny. test-writer addition does not weaken this.
Path-prefix protection shared across roles; test-writer denied_write_path_prefixes MUST include all 10 entries to maintain Feb-13-2026 lock.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: After the test-writer subagent completes, verify on disk that `tests/generated/manifest.json` AND `docs/dev/test-writer-report-<task_id>.json` BOTH exist. If either is missing, abort the cycle with an error
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Hard gate against silent test-writer skip. Cycle 2 EXPANDS this gate to require BOTH root index AND per-task manifest AND report — NOT removed.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Unconditional scan (exception to --changed-files scoping): Standard 6 runs against the full agents and commands directories on every invocation
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Hard guarantee that Standard 6 scope is the full agent/command surface. Cycle 2 exemption text does NOT remove this — it narrows what counts as a violation, not what gets scanned.

Strict-mode shell guard. venv-activation prefix MUST preserve this — `source` does not exit on failure under -e, but `&&` chains DO short-circuit, so `source ... && python3 ...` will fail-fast on activation failure.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: set -euo pipefail (assumed; consistent with score-inject.sh style)

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Zero-stdout invariant for the SessionStart hook to not pollute prompt cache. Cycle 2 mktemp/trap MUST preserve this — trap on EXIT runs even when exec >/dev/null is active.

attempts.proposed_solution: Implement all 6 spec Section 5 components (5.1-5.6) in a single COMPLEX-tier dev cycle.
attempts.actual_change: Created 6 new files (5 scripts + 1 agent) + modified 5 existing files (agents/ba.md, agents/dev.md, agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, commands/close.md) + registered SessionStart hook in settings.json + added agent-scores.json with 21 agents.
attempts.outcome: QA: 18/18 ACs PASS. /close: CLOSE:NO. 3 failure axes captured in spec Section 6: (i) test-writer unconsumable on first dispatch (tool-policy missing + manifest drift); (ii) gitignore cleanliness — 3 runtime artifact patterns leaking; (iii) 12 style-inspector critical findings + 1 Standard 6 Chinese-language escalation.
attempts.target_layer: L5 (created scripts, hooks, new agent infrastructure) + L4 (event delta maps, manifest schema, blast-radius algorithm)

novelty_check.this_attempt_layer: L5 (tool-policy.v1.json role addition + .gitignore + indices regen) + L4 (manifest contract reconciliation between test-writer.md and qa.md, with QA verification scoped to current task_id per codex F4) + L3 (script venv prefix edits, decimal-numbering renumber, /tmp -> mktemp -d + EXIT trap, narrow Standard 6 exemption text)
novelty_check.rationale: Cycle 1 CREATED the harness extension artifacts (5 scripts + 1 agent + agent-scores.json + ba.md/dev.md/qa.md/dev.md(cmd)/close.md additions). Cycle 2 RECONCILES the integration boundaries Cycle 1 introduced: (1) tool-policy authority grant Cycle 1 missed (different file: policies/tool-policy.v1.json — Cycle 1 did not touch this file at all); (2) manifest contract reconciliation between test-writer.md and qa.md (Cycle 1 created the schema but the qa.md verification text used the wrong shape; Cycle 2 fixes the cross-file consistency, not the schema); (3) repo hygiene + style standards Cycle 1's AC set did not cover (different files: .gitignore + 3 indices + style-inspector.md — none of these were in Cycle 1's files_to_modify). The only Cycle-1-touched files Cycle 2 re-enters are commands/dev.md (renumber only; logic unchanged), scripts/score-inject.sh + scripts/score-update.sh + scripts/canary-verify.sh (venv prefix only; logic unchanged), agents/qa.md (Phase 5 schema only; the rest unchanged), and agents/test-writer.md (5 procedural mentions; schema unchanged). No overlap with Cycle 1's logic content.

development_approach.strategy: Apply 8 surgical edits to 12 files in the order: (1) policies/tool-policy.v1.json (P1.1 — JSON validity-protected; do first to make subsequent edits possible from any future test-writer dispatch); (2) agents/test-writer.md procedural sentences (P1.2-a) + agents/qa.md Phase 5 logic+schema (P1.2-b) + commands/dev.md test-writer/Dev dispatch prompts (P1.2-c); (3) .gitignore append (P2.3 — must precede P2.4 per codex F7); (4) regen README.md + INDEX.md + .claude/INDEX.md (P2.4); (5) scripts/score-inject.sh:43 + scripts/score-update.sh:70 venv prefix (here-doc form); (6) scripts/canary-verify.sh:95/106/125 venv prefix (piped form: source ... && echo | python3 ...) per codex F8; (7) commands/dev.md Sub-step 12.1 -> Step 12a + :1110 cross-ref; (8) scripts/canary-verify.sh:79/103 mktemp -d + EXIT trap per codex F9; (9) agents/style-inspector.md narrow Standard 6 exemption with task-id 20260519-211515 + spec citation per codex F10.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA Cycle 2: run the 8 Cycle-2 ACs from docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260519-211515.json + spot-check Cycle 1's 6 representative smoke-test outputs (AC-01, AC-02, AC-14, AC-15, AC-17, AC-18) per spec Section 3 to confirm no regression.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 10 findings, all with PROPOSED_FIX. All applied to spec + AC JSON + blast-radius map.

findings.issue: P1.1 denied list 'mirrors other non-core roles' would block test-writer's own purpose
findings.proposed_fix: Mirror only protected/source denies; do NOT include /tests/
findings.rationale: Verbatim accepted — would have bricked the new role.

findings.issue: P1.1 glob forms tests/generated/** may not match absolute normalized paths
findings.proposed_fix: Use */-prefixed forms matching existing policy entries
findings.rationale: Confirmed by reading existing ba/qa role entries — all use */prefix form.

findings.issue: P1.2 'test-writer.md unchanged' false: lines 139/181/183/198/216 still cite root manifest as active
findings.rationale: Confirmed by reading test-writer.md; schema block (80-127) is correct but procedural sentences drift.

findings.issue: P1.2 'iterate per-task manifests' could regress on stale tests
findings.proposed_fix: QA reads root index to locate CURRENT task_id, verifies only current task's per-task manifest, pytest -q scoped to tests/generated/<task_id>/

findings.issue: P1.2 commands/dev.md must verify both manifests + report
findings.proposed_fix: Update completion check to require all three artifacts
findings.rationale: Logical completion of the contract change.

findings.issue: P1.2 QA output schema still points manifest_verification.manifest_path at root
findings.rationale: Required for QA to report correctly.

findings.issue: P2.4 regen alone may not drop leaks if regen walks filesystem
findings.proposed_fix: Append .gitignore FIRST, then regen; verify no leaks remain

findings.issue: P3.6 piped invocations: `echo $payload | source ... && python3 hook` sends payload to source
findings.proposed_fix: For canary: `source ... && echo $payload | python3 hook`; for heredoc: `source ... && python3 - <<PYEOF`
findings.rationale: Critical correctness — without this fix canary tests would silently break.

findings.issue: P3.8 ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/fixed-name still has collision + cleanup risk
P3.9 broad exemption could mask real violations
findings.proposed_fix: Narrow exemption: only literal user-facing labels/messages with spec/task citation; comments/diagnostics stay English
findings.rationale: Spec wording in AC-C2-08 now requires the narrow form + citation.

findings.issue: P4 scope guard against Section 7 parentheticals luring expansion
findings.proposed_fix: Explicit prohibitions list in spec body
findings.rationale: blast-radius-map.json p4_deferred_explicit_prohibitions[] enumerates the 5 deferred items.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 10 findings + 1 scope guard applied: tool-policy denied list narrowed; glob forms corrected; test-writer.md procedural updates added to scope; QA scoped to current task_id; commands/dev.md verification expanded to both manifests + report; QA schema fields added; .gitignore-first ordering enforced; stdin pipe form preserved; mktemp -d + EXIT trap; narrow Standard 6 exemption with citation; P4 explicit prohibitions in blast-radius-map.
